message.content: Adversaries may disrupt system and network resource availability by inhibiting legitimate user access through Account Access Removal. This involves deleting, locking, or manipulating user accounts, such as changing credentials, and may include logging off users or performing system shutdowns/reboots to enforce changes. In Windows, tools like Net utility, Set-LocalUser, and Set-ADAccountPassword PowerShell cmdlets are commonly used, while in Linux, the passwd utility is employed. Group Policy can also disable accounts. Ransomware groups often use this technique alongside other impact behaviors like Data Destruction and Defacement before encrypting data. Notable examples include LAPSUS$ removing global admin accounts, LockerGoga changing passwords and logging off users, MegaCortex altering passwords and logging off users, and Meteor changing local user passwords and logging off users. Mitigation strategies include implementing strong account management policies, regular monitoring of account activities, and ensuring robust backup and recovery procedures to restore access quickly.
